title: Property-profile contract 1.3.0 (additive): reproducibility.staleness object, correlation_id description refresh, open-schema key documentation
objective: Additive contract 1.3.0 on packages/contracts property_profile schema + services/api emission. (1) Add a typed reproducibility.staleness object (served_from_cache, stale, upstream_error_type, original_retrieved_at, age_seconds) as the machine-readable channel for the M1-T009 last-known-good staleness that is currently a connector_notes convention (M1-T009 G1 findings D1/D2, LOW); keep the human-readable note. (2) Refresh the stale correlation_id description sentence flagged by M1-T009 G1 D2. (3) Document the open-schema derivation and mapped-feature keys flagged by M2-T002 G3 N3. Regenerate TypeScript types through the typegen pipeline and keep the contracts-typegen and contracts-schema-bundle CI jobs byte-identical; move the declared-vs-emitted version check to 1.3.0 atomically with the builder. Additive-optional only: the 1.2.0-shaped client must keep validating. Intended producer: backend-engineer.
business_reason: Session-9 handoff queue item 3: G1-reviewed follow-ups from M1-T009 (D1/D2) and M2-T002 (N3); staleness must be visible in a typed contract field, not a notes convention, before any client renders resilience output
inputs: project-control/reports/M1-T009-G1-data-contract-review.md findings D1/D2 VERBATIM (staleness object shape + correlation_id description staleness)
project-control/reports/M2-T002-G3-human-journey-review.md N3 (open-schema derivation/mapped-feature keys undocumented; bridged today by disclosed runtime-narrowing helpers)
packages/contracts/schemas/v1/property_profile.schema.json at 1.2.0 + packages/contracts/generated/property_profile.ts + contracts-typegen and contracts-schema-bundle CI jobs (M2-T003 outputs)
services/api/app/resilience/** last-known-good staleness convention (served_from_last_known_good connector_notes, M1-T009) and the M2-T003 builder declared-version logic
outputs: property_profile.schema.json 1.3.0: additive optional reproducibility.staleness object (served_from_cache, stale, upstream_error_type, original_retrieved_at, age_seconds); refreshed correlation_id description; documented derivation/mapped-feature open-schema keys
services/api emission populates staleness on LKG/cache serves; connector_notes note retained for humans; declared-vs-emitted check moves to 1.3.0 atomically
Regenerated packages/contracts/generated/property_profile.ts via the typegen pipeline (byte-identical in CI)
Fixture updates + deterministic tests for stale and fresh serves passing boundary validation
allowed_paths: apps/web/src/lib/contract.ts (AMENDMENT A1 2026-07-17: runtime supported-versions vocabulary addition of 1.3.0 ONLY - the client pins a closed SUPPORTED_CONTRACT_VERSIONS set that would reject 1.3.0 payloads at runtime and break the real-builder e2e harness; discovered by the producer per the original STOP condition, packet amended by the orchestrator instead of a follow-up packet because the version bump is atomic by design)
apps/web/src/lib/__tests__/validate-profile.test.ts (AMENDMENT A1: the version-pin assertion update required by the same vocabulary change ONLY)
forbidden_paths: apps/web/** EXCEPT the two A1-named files above (any further client change required means STOP and report for a follow-up packet; components, screens, hooks, styles, e2e specs remain forbidden)
docs/** (research citations belong in the producer report)
acceptance_scenarios: S1 schema: 1.3.0 validates; contracts CI job green; typegen + schema-bundle byte-identical after regeneration

S2 stale serve: LKG path emits staleness (served_from_cache true, stale true, upstream_error_type, original_retrieved_at, age_seconds) and passes boundary validation; the human-readable STALE connector note is retained
S3 fresh serve: staleness truthfully reflects a non-stale serve per the schema definition (or is absent where the schema defines absence); no invented values
S4 version: builder declares 1.3.0; declared-vs-emitted check enforces it; bounded unsupported_contract_version behavior unchanged

S5 compatibility: 1.2.0-shaped payload fixtures still validate against 1.3.0 (additive-optional proof); generated-type client consumers compile without edits beyond the two A1 vocabulary/pin files; web-e2e journeys against the real-builder harness stay green with the builder declaring 1.3.0
S6 correlation_id: refreshed description matches actual generation/propagation behavior (doc text asserted against the code path in review)
S7 regression: full api pytest suite green; connector field mappings untouched (S9 mapping stays as G1-verified)
risks: A1 OVERLAP DOCUMENTATION (2026-07-17): the two A1 client files sit inside M2-T005's apps/web/** scope. Overlap resolution: M2-T005 (a11y defects) has no reason to touch contract.ts or validate-profile.test.ts; the orchestrator verifies at M2-T005 review time that its diff does NOT include these two files, and applies the standard post-first-merge reconciliation (update second branch from main, rerun full CI, prove reviewed-scope diff unchanged) regardless of merge order. If M2-T005's diff DOES touch either file, sequence the merges and obtain delta re-review instead of allowing silent cross-task contamination
Version bump touches M2-T003 declared-vs-emitted enforcement: schema, builder, and tests must move atomically in one PR
age_seconds needs a clock source: use the same injected-clock discipline as M1-T009 (no wall-clock in deterministic tests)

progress_log.message: Session 11 dispatch: G0 PASS (readiness report committed), claimed by backend-engineer, worktree .claude/worktrees/M2-T006 branch task/M2-T006-contract-1-3-0 from main 2a996a3. Scope packages/contracts/** + services/api/**; apps/web forbidden; atomic 1.3.0 move in one PR.

AMENDMENT A1 (orchestrator scope decision): producer triggered the packet STOP condition before implementation - the 1.3.0 declared-version move requires two client edits because apps/web/src/lib/contract.ts pins a closed SUPPORTED_CONTRACT_VERSIONS runtime set (1.0.0/1.1.0/1.2.0) and validate-profile.ts fail-closes on it; the web-e2e harness serves REAL builder output, so a 1.3.0-declaring builder would be client-rejected and CI red with no in-scope fix. Packet amended: allowed_paths widened by EXACTLY apps/web/src/lib/contract.ts (vocabulary addition only) and apps/web/src/lib/__tests__/validate-profile.test.ts (pin update only); S5 and risks updated; overlap with M2-T005 documented with verification-at-review-time rule. Producer report (requested blocked) preserved; producer resumes under amended packet. Systemic follow-up noted: future version publications remain coordinated schema+backend+client changes while the client pins a closed set.
